tasks.prompt: Assess the 2026 DRAM/HBM competitive landscape and the HBM3E->HBM4 transition: who leads, the key technology shifts, and the main supply and geopolitical risks.

rubric.description: SK hynix leads HBM with roughly half or more of the market.
HBM4 enters mass production in 2026 (11+ Gbps, >2 TB/s, 12/16-hi hybrid bonding).
HBM3E remains the flagship, ~two-thirds of 2026 HBM shipments.
DDR5 at 6400 MT/s is mainstream; DDR4 production was extended on price inversion.
Supply/geopolitical risk: China (CXMT) ramp and US export controls.
Custom HBM with a logic base die on an advanced foundry node (TSMC).
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
Intel leads the HBM market and HBM4 is slower than the prior generation.

demo.fused: SK hynix leads HBM with roughly half or more of the market. HBM4 enters mass production in 2026 (11+ Gbps, >2 TB/s, 12/16-hi hybrid bonding). HBM3E remains the flagship, ~two-thirds of 2026 HBM shipments. DDR5 at 6400 MT/s is mainstream; DDR4 production was extended on price inversion. Supply/geopolitical risk: China (CXMT) ramp and US export controls. Custom HBM with a logic base die on an advanced foundry node (TSMC). Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
demo.baseline: SK hynix leads HBM with roughly half or more of the market. HBM4 enters mass production in 2026 (11+ Gbps, >2 TB/s, 12/16-hi hybrid bonding). Intel leads the HBM market and HBM4 is slower than the prior generation.
demo.panel: SK hynix leads HBM with roughly half or more of the market. HBM4 enters mass production in 2026 (11+ Gbps, >2 TB/s, 12/16-hi hybrid bonding). Intel leads the HBM market and HBM4 is slower than the prior generation. (panel member perspective)

tasks.prompt: Compare the three majors' DRAM process-node and HBM roadmaps and what differentiates their HBM4 approaches.

rubric.description: Micron pushes 1-gamma; Samsung uses 1c DRAM for HBM4.
All three rely on EUV at the leading edge.
HBM4 uses hybrid bonding for 12/16-hi stacks.
Yield ramp is the key differentiator on HBM4.
SK hynix pairs the HBM4 logic base die with a TSMC foundry node.
Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
DDR4 is fully discontinued by all vendors.

demo.fused: Micron pushes 1-gamma; Samsung uses 1c DRAM for HBM4. All three rely on EUV at the leading edge. HBM4 uses hybrid bonding for 12/16-hi stacks. Yield ramp is the key differentiator on HBM4. SK hynix pairs the HBM4 logic base die with a TSMC foundry node. Primary sources: https://example.org/report [1].
demo.baseline: Micron pushes 1-gamma; Samsung uses 1c DRAM for HBM4. HBM4 uses hybrid bonding for 12/16-hi stacks. DDR4 is fully discontinued by all vendors.
demo.panel: Micron pushes 1-gamma; Samsung uses 1c DRAM for HBM4. HBM4 uses hybrid bonding for 12/16-hi stacks. DDR4 is fully discontinued by all vendors. (panel member perspective)

tasks.prompt: Plan SK hynix's HBM4 capacity ramp quarter-by-quarter from 2026 H1 to 2027 H1: wafer allocation between HBM and general DRAM, packaging/hybrid-bonding capacity, customer commitments (NVIDIA Rubin), capex phasing, and a contingency for a 2027 oversupply risk. Keep the plan internally consistent across quarters.

rubric.description: Allocates wafers between HBM (uses ~3x wafers) and general DRAM.
Ramps advanced packaging / hybrid-bonding capacity.
Secures customer commitments (NVIDIA Rubin).
Phases capex across the M15X / new fabs.
Includes a 2027 oversupply contingency.
Later reallocates all wafers to general DRAM (contradicts the HBM plan).

any: reallocates all wafers to general DRAM

demo.fused: Allocates wafers between HBM (uses ~3x wafers) and general DRAM. Ramps advanced packaging / hybrid-bonding capacity. Secures customer commitments (NVIDIA Rubin). Phases capex across the M15X / new fabs. Includes a 2027 oversupply contingency.
demo.baseline: Allocates wafers between HBM (uses ~3x wafers) and general DRAM. Ramps advanced packaging / hybrid-bonding capacity. Secures customer commitments (NVIDIA Rubin). Includes a 2027 oversupply contingency.
demo.panel: Allocates wafers between HBM (uses ~3x wafers) and general DRAM. Ramps advanced packaging / hybrid-bonding capacity. Secures customer commitments (NVIDIA Rubin). Includes a 2027 oversupply contingency. (panel member perspective)
